_comment: Declarative policy for how the wizard applies the shipped build-config (userdata/) onto a device. Bump config_version whenever ANY file in userdata/ changes -- that is what makes the wizard detect and apply the new config. 'fresh' is used on a clean/first install, 'update' on an existing device. Modes: replace (overwrite whole file), merge_id (per <setting id=...>, build value wins, other user settings untouched), merge_name (per <source><name>, add build's, keep user's), seed_if_absent (write only if the file does not already exist).

files._note: POV's own settings. WAS bundled in config.zip but never in this policy, so it was never applied -- POV ran on its English/no-fanart defaults (the black-background + English-metadata bug). fresh=replace lands our full config (meta_language=he, get_fanart_data=true, TMDB/Trakt app keys, provider toggles). update=merge_id pushes those build defaults to existing devices WITHOUT clobbering the user's own service logins -- every credential / per-account id is in exclude_ids so My Services authorizations (Real-Debrid, Trakt, Premiumize, TorBox, ...) survive a config update.
favourites.xml holds ONLY internal skin shortcuts (the build is Trakt/TMDB-driven), so overwriting it on update is safe and lets UI/shortcut/icon updates reach existing users.

Seeded POV navigator state (custom menu order/visibility). Binary DB -- shipped whole and OVERWRITTEN on both fresh and update so build menu changes always reach users. No merge at this stage (by design).
Seeded POV per-list view types. Binary DB -- overwritten on fresh + update.

Seeded FENtastic home-view / main-menu + widget path cache. Binary DB -- intentionally shipped (not a per-device-only cache here) and overwritten on fresh + update to pin the build's home layout and fix widget bugs.

cleanup._comment: Twilight is dead and replaced by POV. Remove stale Twilight userdata leftovers so they cannot resurrect menus/settings. Paths are relative to special://home/.
